healthcare.title: PMS Scheduling Screen
healthcare.description: The core objective of this redesign was to transform a high-risk administrative interface into a safety-first scheduling engine. By applying NIST Human Factors principles, I addressed critical vulnerabilities in the original state—specifically high cognitive load.
healthcare.goal: To set up and manage complex clinical schedules for multiple doctors without causing booking errors.
healthcare.environment: A fast-paced clinic where the user is frequently interrupted by phone calls or patient check-ins.

healthcare.title: MentalHealth

healthcare.title: Provider Credentialing & Enrollment
healthcare.description: A redesign of three core screens in a healthcare credentialing system — showing how targeted UX decisions reduced cognitive load and made critical compliance information impossible to miss.

healthcare.title: Conceptual Project
healthcare.tagLine: Real OPD workflows and healthcare UX constraints
healthcare.description: It's how OPD queues work everywhere. The problem isn't the wait itself — it's the uncertainty. Patients can't leave, can't rest, can't plan. They're hostages to an opaque system. I decided to design the system that should have existed
